notes.note: Cicero's rhetorical treatise the Orator, on the ideal orator, completed in 46 BC and dedicated to Brutus; here he has commended a copy to Trebonius's friend Sabinus.

Cures was the chief town of the Sabines, an old people proverbial for plain, austere uprightness; Cicero teases that Sabinus's grave manner suited his Sabine surname, while wondering whether he had simply adopted the flattering name.

notes.anchor: where you have seen our Brutus
notes.note: Marcus Junius Brutus, the future tyrannicide; Trebonius's route in April 44 BC, on his way to his province of Asia, was expected to bring him into contact with Brutus.
